questions.prompt: Where does the system prompt go in a Claude API call?

choices.text: As a `{role: 'system', content: ...}` entry in the messages array.
As a top-level `system` field on the request, separate from `messages`. The messages array only contains `user` and `assistant` turns.

questions.explanation: Claude's API has a dedicated top-level `system` field. The `messages` array only contains user/assistant turns, must alternate, and must start with `user`. Putting the system prompt as a role inside messages is one of the most common Claude API mistakes for people coming from OpenAI's chat completions.

questions.prompt: Your agent is calling tools but Claude keeps repeating the same tool call. You're storing only `response.content[0].text` in conversation history. What's wrong?

choices.text: Tool use doesn't work with Claude.

You're dropping non-text content blocks. `response.content` is an array — text, tool_use, and other typed blocks. Always append the full `response.content` array to history, not just the first block's text. Otherwise Claude loses track of what it already tried to do.
Tool calls don't go in history.

questions.explanation: Claude responses are arrays of typed blocks. A response can include both a `text` block and a `tool_use` block. Storing only the text loses the tool call data, so on the next turn Claude doesn't know it already attempted the tool. Fix: always store the entire `response.content` array.
